chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Over the last few years, AI systems have become astonishingly good at turning text prompts

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: into videos. At the core of how these models operate is a deep connection to physics. This

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: generation of image and video models works using a process known as diffusion, which is remarkably

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: equivalent to the Brownian motion we see as particles diffuse, but with time run backwards

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and in high dimensional space. As we'll see, this connection to physics is much more than

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a curiosity. We get real algorithms out of the physics that we can use to generate images

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and videos. And this perspective will also give us some really nice intuitions for how

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: these models work in practice. But before we dive into this connection, let's get hands

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: on with a real diffusion model. While the best models are closed source, there are some compelling

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: open source models. This video of an astronaut was generated by an open source model called

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: WAN 2.1. We can add to our prompt and have our astronaut hold a flag, hold a laptop, or hold

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a meeting. If we cut down our prompt to just an astronaut, we get this. And if we cut down

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our prompt to nothing, we interestingly still get this video of a woman. If we dig into our

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: WAN model's source code, we'll find that the video generation process begins with this

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: call to a random number generator, creating a video where the pixel intensity values are

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: chosen randomly. Here's what it looks like. From here, this pure noise video is passed into

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a transformer. This is the same type of AI model used by large language models like ChatGPT.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But instead of outputting text, this transformer outputs another video that now looks like this.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Still mostly noise, but with some hints of structure. This new video is added to our pure noise video.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And then passed back into the model again, producing a third video that looks like this.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This process is repeated again and again. Here's what the video looks like after five iterations.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: 10, 20, 30, 40, and finally 50.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Step by step, our transformer shapes pure noise into incredibly realistic video.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But what exactly is the connection to Brownian motion here? And how is our model able to use text

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: inputs so expressively to shape noise into what our prompt describes? In this video, we'll unpack diffusion models in three parts.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: First, we'll look at a 2021 OpenAI paper and model called CLIP. As we'll see, CLIP is really two models,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a language model and a vision model that are trained using a clever learning objective that allows them to learn this really powerful shared space between words and pictures.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Experimenting with this space will help us get a feel for the high dimensional spaces that diffusion models operate in.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But learning a shared representation is not enough to generate images. From here, we'll look at the diffusion process itself.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: At a high level, diffusion models are trained to remove noise from images or videos.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, if you dig into the landmark papers in the field, you'll find that this naive understanding of diffusion really doesn't hold up in practice.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: In this section, we'll dig into the connection between diffusion models and diffusion processes in physics.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This connection will help us understand how these models really work in practice and give us some powerful theory for dramatically speeding up image and video generation.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Finally, we'll bring these worlds together and see how approaches like CLIP are combined with diffusion models to condition and guide the generation process towards the videos we asked for in our prompts.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: 2020 was a landmark year for language modeling.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: New results in neural scaling laws and OpenAI's GPT-3 showed that bigger really was better.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Massive models trained on massive datasets had capabilities that simply didn't exist in smaller models.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: It didn't take long for researchers to apply similar ideas to images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: In February 2021, a team at OpenAI released a new model architecture called CLIP, trained on a dataset of 400 million image and caption pairs scraped from the internet.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: CLIP is composed of two models, one that processes text and one that processes images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The output of each of these models is a vector of length 512.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And the central idea is that the vectors for a given image and its caption should be similar.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: To achieve this, the OpenAI team developed a clever training approach.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Given a batch of image-caption pairs, for example, our batch could contain a picture of a cat, a dog, and me, with the captions a photo of a cat, a photo of a dog, and a photo of a man.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We then pass our three images into our image model, and our three captions into our text model.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We now have three image vectors and three text vectors.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And we would like the vectors for the matching image-caption pairs to be similar.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The clever idea from here is to make use of the similarity not just between the corresponding images and captions,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but between all image-caption pairs in the batch when training our models.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we arrange our image vectors as the columns of a matrix, and our text vectors as the rows,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the pairs of vectors along the diagonal of our matrix correspond to matching images and captions,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and all the pairs off-diagonal are non-matching images and captions.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The CLIP training objective seeks to maximize the similarity between corresponding image-caption pairs,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: while simultaneously minimizing the similarity between non-corresponding image-caption pairs.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The C in CLIP stands for contrastive, because the model learns to contrast matching and non-matching image-caption pairs.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The CLIP algorithm measures similarity between vectors using a metric called cosine similarity.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Geometrically, we can think of each of these vectors as pointing in some direction in high-dimensional space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Cosine similarity measures the cosine of the angle between our vectors in this space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So if our text and image vector point in the same direction, the angle between our vectors will be zero,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: resulting in a maximum value for our cosine similarity score of one.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So the image and text models that make up CLIP are trained to maximize the alignment of related images and captions

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: in this shared high-dimensional space, while minimizing the alignment between unrelated images and captions.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The learned geometry of this shared vector space, known as a latent or embedding space,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: has some really interesting properties.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If I take two pictures of myself, one not wearing a hat and one wearing a hat,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and pass both of these into our CLIP image model, we get two vectors in our embedding space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now if I take the vector corresponding to me wearing a hat, and subtract the vector of me not wearing a hat,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we get a new vector in our embedding space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now what text might this new vector correspond to?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Mathematically, we took the difference of me wearing a hat and me not wearing a hat.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can search for corresponding text by passing a bunch of different words into our text encoder,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and for each computing the cosine similarity between our newly computed difference vector and the text vector.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Testing a set of a few hundred common words, the top-ranked match with a similarity of 0.165 is the word hat,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: followed by cap and helmet.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This is a remarkable result.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The learned geometry of CLIP's embedding space allows us to operate mathematically on the pure ideas or concepts in our images and text.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Translating the differences in the content of our images, like if there's a hat or not,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: into a literal distance between vectors in our embedding space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The OpenAI team showed that CLIP could produce very impressive image classification results,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: by simply passing an image into our image encoder,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and then comparing the resulting vector to a set of possible captions,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: one for each label that could be assigned to the image,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and classifying the image with whatever label resulted in the highest cosine similarity.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So techniques like CLIP give us a powerful shared representation of image and text,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a kind of vector space of pure ideas.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, our CLIP models only go one direction.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can only map image and text to our shared embedding space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We have no way of generating images and text from our embedding vectors.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: 2020 turned out not only to be a transformative year for language modeling.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: A few weeks after the GPT-3 paper came out,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: a team at Berkeley published a paper called

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: now known as DDPM.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The paper showed for the first time that it was possible to generate very high quality images

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: using a diffusion process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where pure noise is transformed step by step into realistic images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The core idea behind diffusion models is pretty straightforward.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We take a set of training images and add noise to each image step by step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: until the image is completely destroyed.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: From here we train a neural network to reverse this process.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: When I first learned about diffusion models,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: I assumed that the models would be trained to remove noise a single step at a time.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Our model would be trained to predict the image in step 1 given the noisier image in step 2,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: trained to predict the image in step 2 given the noisier image in step 3, and so on.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: When it came time to generate an image, we would pass pure noise into our model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: take its output and pass it back into its input again and again,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and after enough steps we would have a nice image.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, it turns out that this naive approach to building a diffusion model really does not work well.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Virtually no modern models work like this.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: These are the training and image generation algorithms from the Berkeley team's paper.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The notation is a bit dense, but there's some key details we can pull out

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: that will help us understand what it takes to make these models really work.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The first thing that surprised me is that the team added random noise to images

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: not just during training, but also during image generation.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Algorithm 2 tells us that when generating new images, at each step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: after our neural network predicts a less noisy image,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we need to add random noise to this image before passing it back into our model.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This added noise turns out to matter a lot in practice.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we take a popular diffusion model like stable diffusion 2,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and use the Berkeley team's image generation approach, known as DDPM sampling,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we can get some really nice images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Here's the image we get when prompting the model with this prompt,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: asking for a tree in the desert.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, if we remove the line of code that adds noise at each step of the generation process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we end up with a tiny, sad, blurry tree.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: How is it that adding random noise while generating images leads to better quality, sharper images?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The second thing that surprised me when I encountered the Berkeley team's approach

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: was that the team wasn't training models to reverse a single step in the noise addition process.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Instead, the team takes an initial clean image, which they call x0,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and adds scaled random noise to the image, which they call epsilon.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And from here they train the model to predict the total noise that was added to the original image.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So the team is effectively asking the model to skip all the intermediate steps,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and make a prediction about the original image.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Intuitively, this learning task seems much more difficult to me

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: than just learning to make a noisy image slightly less noisy.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The Berkeley team's paper and approach was a landmark result that put diffusion on the map.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Why does adding random noise while generating images, and training the model like this, work so well?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The DDPM paper draws on some fairly complex theory to arrive at these algorithms.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: I'll include a link to a great tutorial in the description if you want to dig deeper into the theory.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Happily, it turns out that there's a different, but mathematically equivalent, way of understanding

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: what diffusion models are really learning, that we can use to get a visual and intuitive sense

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: for why the DDPM algorithms work so well.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The key will be thinking of diffusion models as learning a time-varying vector field.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This perspective also leads to a more general approach, called flow-based models,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: which have become very popular recently.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: To see how diffusion models learn this time-varying vector field,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: let's temporarily simplify our learning problem.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: One way to think about an image is as a point in high-dimensional space,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where the intensity value of each pixel controls the position of the point in each dimension.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we reduce the size of our images to only two pixels,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we can visualize the distribution of our images by plotting the pixel intensity value

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: of our first pixel on the x-axis of a scatter plot,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and the pixel intensity of our second pixel on the y-axis.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So an image with a black first pixel and a white second pixel would show up at x equals 0,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and y equals 1 on our scatter plot.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And an all-white image would be at 1, 1, and so on.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, real images have a very specific structure in this high-dimensional space.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Let's create some structure for our points in our lower two-dimensional space

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: for our diffusion model to learn.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The exact structure we choose doesn't matter too much at this point.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Let's start with a spiral shape like this.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The core idea of diffusion models, adding more and more noise to an image

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and then training a neural network to reverse this process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: looks really interesting from the perspective of our 2D toy data.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: When we add random noise to an image,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we're effectively changing each pixel's value by a random amount.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: In our toy 2D dataset,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where the coordinates of a point correspond to that image's pixel intensity values,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: adding random noise is equivalent to taking a step in a randomly chosen direction.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: As we add more and more noise to our image, our point goes on a random walk.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This process is equivalent to the Brownian motion that drives diffusion processes in physics,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and is where diffusion models get their name.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: From here, it's pretty wild to think about what we're asking our diffusion model to do.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Our model will see many different random walks from various starting points in our dataset,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and we're effectively asking our model to reverse the clock,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: removing noise from our images by learning to play these diffusion processes backwards,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: starting our points from random locations and recovering the original structure of our dataset.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: How can our model learn to reverse these random walks?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we consider the specific point at the end of this 100-step random walk,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: in our naive diffusion modeling approach,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where we ask our model to denoise images a single step at a time,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: this is equivalent to giving our model the coordinates of the final 100th point in our walk,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and asking our model to predict the coordinates of our point at the 99th step.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Although the direction of our 100th step is chosen randomly,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: there will be some signal in aggregate for our model to learn from here.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Given enough training points,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we expect many diffusion paths to go through this neighborhood,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and on average our points will be diffusing away from our starting spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: so our model can learn to point back towards our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can now see why the Berkeley team's training objective works so well.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Instead of training the model to remove noise from images one step at a time,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: this would correspond to predicting the coordinates of the 99th step given the 100th,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the team instead trained the model to predict the total noise added across the entire walk.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: On our plot, this is the vector pointing from our 100th step back to the original starting point of the walk.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: It turns out that we can prove that learning to predict the noise added in the final step of our walk

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: is mathematically equivalent to learning to predict the total noise added,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: divided by the number of steps taken.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This means that when our model learns to reverse a single step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: although our training data is noisy,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we expect our model to ultimately learn to point back towards x0.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: By instead training our model to directly predict the vector pointing back towards x0,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we're significantly reducing the variance of our training examples,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: allowing our model to learn much more efficiently,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: without actually changing our underlying learning objective.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So for each point in our space,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our model learns the direction pointing back towards the original data distribution.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This is also known as a score function.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And the intuition here is that the score function points us towards more likely, less noisy data.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, in practice, these learned directions depend heavily on how much noise we add to our original data.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: After 100 steps, most of our points are far from their starting points.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So our model learns to move these points back in the general direction of our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, if we train our model on examples after only one diffusion step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we end up with a much more nuanced vector field, pointing to the fine structure of our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: There turns out to be a clever solution to this problem.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Instead of just passing in the coordinates of our point into our model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: which we'll write here as a function f,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we can also pass in a time variable that corresponds to the number of steps taken in our random walk.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we set t equal to 1 at our 100th step, then t would equal 0.99 at our 99th step, and so on.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Conditioning our models on time like this turns out to be essential in practice,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: allowing our model to learn coarse vector fields for large values of t,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and very refined structures as t approaches 0.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: After training, we can watch the time evolution of our model.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We see this really interesting behavior as t approaches 0.4.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Our learned vector field suddenly transitions,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: from pointing towards the center of the spiral to pointing towards the spiral itself.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: It feels like a phase change.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We're now in a great position to resolve the final mystery of the DDPM paper.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: How is it that adding random noise at each step while generating images leads to better quality, sharper images?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Let's follow the path of a single point guided by the DDPM image generation algorithm.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: On our 2D dataset, generating an image is equivalent to starting at a random location,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and working our way back to our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Starting at a randomly chosen location of x equals minus 1.6 and y equals 1.8,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our model's vector field points us back towards our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Following the DDPM algorithm, we take a small step in the direction returned by our model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and add scaled random noise, which effectively moves our point in a random direction.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We'll color the steps driven by our diffusion model in blue, and our random steps in gray.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Note that the scale of the random step may seem large, but following our DDPM algorithm,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the size of our random steps will come down as we progress.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Repeating this process for 64 steps, our particle jumps around quite a bit,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: due to both our learned vector field changing, and our random noise steps,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but ultimately lands nicely on our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Repeating this process for a point cloud of 256 points,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our reverse diffusion process starts out looking like absolute chaos,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but does converge nicely, with most points landing on our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, what happens if we remove the noise addition steps?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Running our reverse diffusion process again, without the random noise step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: all of our points quickly move to the center of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and then make their way towards a single inside edge of the spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This result can help us make sense of why we saw a sad blurry tree earlier,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: when we removed this random noise step.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Instead of capturing our full spiral distribution,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: as we did when we included a noise step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: all of our generated points end up close to the center or average of our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: In the space of images, averages look blurry.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Conceptually, we can imagine different parts of our spiral corresponding to different images of trees in the desert.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And when we remove the random noise steps from our generation process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our generated images end up in the center or average of these images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: which looks like a blurry mess.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, note that the analogy between our toy dataset and high-dimensional image dataset breaks down a bit here.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If all the points on our spiral correspond to realistic images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: since our generated points do still end up landing on our 2D spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we would expect these generated points to still look like real images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but likely with less diversity than we would want.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, in the high-dimensional space of images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: it appears that our image generation process doesn't quite make it to the manifold of realistic images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: resulting in a blurry, non-realistic image.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This prediction of the average is not a coincidence.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: It turns out that we can show mathematically that our model learns to point to the mean or average of our dataset,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: conditioned on our input point and the time in our diffusion process.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: One way to arrive at this result is to show that given the noise we add in our forward process is Gaussian,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: for sufficiently small step sizes, our reverse process will also follow a Gaussian distribution,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where our model actually learns the mean of this distribution.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Since our model just predicts the mean of our normal distribution,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: to actually sample from this distribution,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we need to add zero-mean Gaussian noise to our model's predicted value,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: which is precisely what the DDPM image generation process does

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: when we add random noise after each step.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can see this mean learning behavior most clearly early in our reverse diffusion process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: when t is close to 1 and our training points are far from our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Our model's learned vector field points towards the center or average of our dataset.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So adding random noise during image generation falls nicely out of theory,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and in practice prevents all our points from landing near the center or average of our dataset.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The DDPM paper put diffusion models on the map as a viable method of generating images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But the diffusion approach did not immediately see widespread adoption.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: A key issue with the DDPM approach at the time was the high compute demands of the large number of steps required to generate high-quality images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: since each step required a complete pass through a potentially very large neural network.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: A few months later, a pair of papers from teams at Stanford and Google

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: showed that it's remarkably possible to generate high-quality images without actually adding random noise during the generation process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: significantly reducing the number of steps required.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The DDPM image generation process we've been looking at can be expressed using a special type of differential equation

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: known as a stochastic differential equation.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This first term represents the motion of our point driven by our model's vector field,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and this second term represents the random motions of our point.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Adding these terms together, we get the overall motion of our point at each step, dx.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: From here, we can consider how the distribution of all of our points evolves over time,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: where the motion of each point is governed by this stochastic differential equation.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This problem has been well studied in physics.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Using a key result from statistical mechanics known as the Fokker-Planck equation,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the Google Brain team showed that there's another differential equation,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: this time an ordinary differential equation with no random component,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: that results in the same exact final distribution of points as our stochastic differential equation.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This result gives us a new algorithm for generating images using our model's learned vector fields,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: that does not require taking random steps along the way.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Exactly how our ordinary differential equation maps to an image generation algorithm is a bit technical.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: I'll leave a link to a tutorial in the description.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The key result here though, is that we end up with something that looks very similar to our DDPM image generation process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but without the random noise addition at each step,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and with a new scaling for the sizes of steps that we take.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This approach is generally known as DDIM.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The scaling of our step sizes, and especially how these step sizes vary throughout our reverse diffusion process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: matters a lot in practice.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: When we just removed the random noise steps from our DDPM generation algorithm earlier,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: all of our points ended up near the mean of our data,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and we saw blurry results for our generated images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Switching to our DDIM approach,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we now have smaller scaling for our step sizes,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: that allow our trajectories to better follow the contour lines of our vector field,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and land nicely on the correct spiral distribution.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And applying our DDIM algorithm to our tree in the desert example,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we're now able to generate nice results.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Comparing to our original DDPM algorithm that required random steps,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: DDIM remarkably does not require any changes to model training,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but is able to generate high quality images in significantly fewer steps,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: completely deterministically.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Note that the theory does not tell us that our individual images or points on our spiral will be the same,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but instead that our final distribution of points or images will be the same,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: regardless of whether we use our stochastic DDPM algorithm or our deterministic DDIM algorithm.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The Wan model we saw earlier uses a generalization of DDIM called flow matching.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: By early 2021, it was clear that diffusion models were capable of generating high quality images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And thanks to image generation methods like DDIM,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: it was possible to generate these images without using enormous amounts of compute.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, our ability to steer the diffusion process using text prompts was still very limited.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Earlier, we saw how CLIP was able to learn a powerful shared representation of images and text

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: by concurrently training image and text encoder models.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: However, these models only go one way, converting text or images into embedding vectors.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: These two problems potentially fit together in a really interesting way.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Diffusion models are able to potentially reverse the CLIP image encoder,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: generating high quality images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And the output vector of the CLIP text encoder could be used to guide our diffusion models

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: toward the images or videos that we want.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: So the high-level idea here is that we could pass in a prompt into the CLIP text encoder

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: to generate an embedding vector, and use this embedding vector to steer the diffusion process

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: towards the image or video of what our prompt describes.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: A team at OpenAI did exactly this in 2022.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Using image and caption pairs to train a diffusion model

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: to invert the CLIP image encoder.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Their approach yielded an incredible level of prompt adherence,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: capturing an unprecedented level of detail from the input text.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The team called their method un-CLIP, but their model is better known by its commercial name, DALI2.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But how do we actually use the embedding vectors for models like CLIP to steer the diffusion process?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: One option is to simply pass our text vector as another input into our diffusion model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and train as we normally would to remove noise.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we train our diffusion model using image and caption pairs, as the OpenAI team did,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the idea here is that the model will learn to use the text information to more accurately remove noise from images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: since it now has more context about the image that it's learning to denoise.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This technique is called conditioning.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We used a similar approach earlier, when we conditioned our toy diffusion model on the number of time steps elapsed in the diffusion process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: allowing the model to learn coarse structure for large values of T,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and finer structures as our training samples get closer to our original spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Interestingly, there turns out to be a variety of ways we can pass in the text vector into our diffusion model.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Some approaches use a mechanism called cross-attention to couple image and text information.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Other approaches simply add or append the embedded text vector to our diffusion model's input,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and some approaches pass in text information in multiple ways at once.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, it turns out that conditioning alone is not enough to achieve the level of prompt adherence that we see in models like DALI2.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we take the stable diffusion tree in the desert example we've been experimenting with,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and only condition our model with our text inputs,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the model no longer gives us everything we ask for.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We get a shadow and a desert, but no tree.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Note that stable diffusion was developed by a team at Heidelberg University around the same time as DALI2,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and works in a similar way, but is open source.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: It turns out that there's one more powerful idea that we need to effectively steer our diffusion models.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can see this idea in action by returning to our toy dataset one last time.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If our overall spiral corresponds to realistic images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: then different sections of our spiral may correspond to different types of images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Let's say this inner part is images of people, this middle part is images of dogs,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and this outer part is different images of cats.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, let's train the same diffusion model we trained earlier,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but in addition to passing in our starting coordinates and the time of our diffusion process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we'll also pass in the points class, person, cat, or dog.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This extra signal should allow our model to steer points to the right sections of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: based on each points class.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Running our generation process, after assigning person, dog, or cat labels to each point,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we see that we're able to recover the overall structure of our dataset,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but the fit is not great, and we see some confusion here between people and dog images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Part of the problem here is that we're asking our model to simultaneously learn to point to our overall spiral of realistic images,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and towards specific classes on our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we consider this cat point, for example,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: it starts off heading towards the center of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and as our class-conditioned vector field shifts to point towards the cat region of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our point moves towards this part of the spiral, but it doesn't quite make it.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The modeling task of generally matching our overall spiral

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: has overpowered our model's ability to move our point in the direction of a specific class.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now, is there a way to decouple and maybe even control these two factors?

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Remarkably, it turns out that we can.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The trick is to leverage the differences between the unconditional model that is not trained on a specific class,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and a model that is conditioned on specific classes.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We could do this by training two separate models,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but in practice it's more efficient to just leave out the class information for a subset of our training examples.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We now have the option of effectively passing a no class or text information into our model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and getting back a vector field that points towards our data in general, not towards any specific class.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can visualize these two vector fields together.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Here, the gray vectors show our diffusion model points when we don't pass in any class information,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and these yellow vectors show when our model is conditioned on the cat class.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: For large values of our diffusion time variable, when our training data is far from our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our two vector fields basically point in the same direction, roughly towards the average of our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: But as time approaches zero, our vector fields diverge, with our cat conditioned vector field pointing more towards the outer cat portion of our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Now that we have these two separate directions, we can use their differences to push our points more in the direction of the class we want.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Specifically, we take our yellow class conditioned vector, and subtract our gray unconditioned vector.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This gives us a new vector pointing from the tip of our unconditioned vector to the tip of our conditioned vector.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The idea from here is that this direction should point more in the direction of our cat examples,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: now that we've removed the direction generally pointing towards our data.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We can now amplify this direction by multiplying by a scaling factor, alpha,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and replace our original conditioned yellow vector with the vector pointing in this new direction.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Let's follow the trajectory of the same cat point we saw earlier that didn't quite make it onto our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: We'll roll back our diffusion time variable, and start a new green point from the same starting location.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we use our new green vectors to guide the diffusion process instead of our original yellow vectors,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the difference between our gray arrows that point towards the center of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and yellow vectors that start pointing us back towards our cat part of the spiral are amplified,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: now guiding our point to land nicely on our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: This approach is called classifier-free guidance.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Using our new green vectors to guide a set of cat points,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we see a nice tight fit to our spiral for this class.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Switching to our dog class, our unconditional gray vector field stays the same,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: but our dog conditioned model outputs shown in magenta now point us more towards the dog part of our spiral,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and adding guidance amplifies this learned direction.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Using our guided vectors and running our generation process,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we see a nice fit for our dog points.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Finally, we get a third vector field for our people examples,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: that again results in nice convergence to our spiral.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Classifier-free guidance works remarkably well,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and has become an essential part of many modern image and video generation models.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Earlier, we saw that if we only conditioned our stable diffusion model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our image would have a desert and a shadow, but no tree that we asked for in the prompt.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: If we add classifier-free guidance to this model,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: once we reach a guidance scale alpha of around 2,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: we start to actually see a tiny tree in our images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: And the size and detail of our tree improve as we increase our scaling factor alpha.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: The fact that this works so well is remarkable to me.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: As we use guidance to point our stable diffusion model's vector field more in the direction of our prompt,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: our tree literally grows in size and detail in our images.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Our WAN video generation model takes this guidance approach one step further.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Instead of subtracting the output of an unconditioned model with no text input,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: the WAN team uses what's known as a negative prompt,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and then subtract the resulting vector from the model's conditioned output,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: and amplify the result,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: steering the diffusion process away from these unwanted features.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: Their standard negative prompt is fascinating.

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
chunks.text: including features like extra fingers and walking backwards,

chunks.title: But how do AI images and videos actually work Guest video by Welch Labs
